perturbation.original_text: 1. APPOINTMENT OF CHANNEL PARTNER 1.1 General. iPass will make the Service available to Channel Partner in exchange for the fees referenced in the Exhibits attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference. Channel Partner may order Services using an iPass approved order form available via the iPass Portal. Each order form must reference this Agreement and each order form shall merge and incorporate the terms herein. Each order form is a separate and distinct contractual obligation.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. APPOINTMENT OF CHANNEL PARTNER 1.1 General. iPass will make the Offering available to Channel Partner in exchange for the fees referenced in the Exhibits attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference. Channel Partner may order Offerings using an iPass approved order form available via the iPass Portal. Each order form must reference this Agreement and each order form shall merge and incorporate the terms herein. Each order form is a separate and distinct contractual obligation.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Service' is a defined term, while 'Offering' is not. By changing 'Service' to 'Offering,' we create confusion because the contract then refers to an undefined 'Offering' instead of the defined 'Service,' potentially altering the scope of what iPass is obligated to provide. This creates an in-text contradiction in section 1.1 as the term 'Service' is reffered to, in the rest of the legal text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. APPOINTMENT OF CHANNEL PARTNER 1.1 General. iPass will make the Offering available to Channel Partner in exchange for the fees referenced in the Exhibits attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference. Channel Partner may order Offerings using an iPass approved order form available via the iPass Portal. Each order form must reference this Agreement and each order form shall merge and incorporate the terms herein. Each order form is a separate and distinct contractual obligation.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Service' to 'Offering' introduces a contradiction because 'Service' is a defined term within the contract, while 'Offering' is not. This misalignment creates ambiguity and directly contradicts the contract's expectation of using defined terms consistently, as the contradicted text refers to 'Offering' without it being properly defined, leading to confusion and potential disputes over the scope of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 2. CHANNEL PARTNER RESPONSIBILITIES 2.1 General. Channel Partner will use its reasonable endeavours cooperate with iPass and assist with the implementation of this Agreement and performance of the Services. Prior to deployment of the Licensed Software in a production environment, Channel Partner agrees to implement processes that will allow the Licensed Software to successfully report as specified in the Documentation. If Channel Partner fails to implement such processes within a reasonable time following iPass' request, iPass may elect to terminate the Agreement or Channel Partner's use of the Licensed Software upon written notice to Channel Partner. In such an event, Channel Partner shall be responsible for payment of all undisputed amounts due and owing to iPass under the Agreement. In addition, Channel Partner agrees to ensure that prior to deployment of the Licensed Software in a Business Entity production environment, each Business Entity implements processes that will allow the Licensed Software to successfully report as specified in the Documentation. If Channel Partner's Business Entity fails to implement such processes within a reasonable time following iPass' or Channel Partner's request, iPass may elect to terminate the order for such Business Entity upon written notice to Channel Partner. In such an event, Channel Partner shall be responsible for payment of all undisputed amounts due and owing to iPass under the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. CHANNEL PARTNER RESPONSIBILITIES 2.1 General. Channel Partner will use its reasonable endeavours cooperate with iPass and assist with the implementation of this Agreement and performance of the Deliverables. Prior to deployment of the Licensed Software in a production environment, Channel Partner agrees to implement processes that will allow the Licensed Software to successfully report as specified in the Documentation. If Channel Partner fails to implement such processes within a reasonable time following iPass' request, iPass may elect to terminate the Agreement or Channel Partner's use of the Licensed Software upon written notice to Channel Partner. In such an event, Channel Partner shall be responsible for payment of all undisputed amounts due and owing to iPass under the Agreement. In addition, Channel Partner agrees to ensure that prior to deployment of the Licensed Software in a Business Entity production environment, each Business Entity implements processes that will allow the Licensed Software to successfully report as specified in the Documentation. If Channel Partner's Business Entity fails to implement such processes within a reasonable time following iPass' or Channel Partner's request, iPass may elect to terminate the order for such Business Entity upon written notice to Channel Partner. In such an event, Channel Partner shall be responsible for payment of all undisputed amounts due and owing to iPass under the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Services' to 'Deliverables,' another undefined term, creates uncertainty. The contract details specific responsibilities tied to 'Services,' but replacing it with 'Deliverables' introduces ambiguity about what Channel Partner is responsible for delivering, and whether the existing responsibilities related to 'Services' still apply. This creates an in-text contradiction in section 2.1 as the term 'Service' is reffered to, in the rest of the legal text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. CHANNEL PARTNER RESPONSIBILITIES 2.1 General. Channel Partner will use its reasonable endeavours cooperate with iPass and assist with the implementation of this Agreement and performance of the Deliverables. Prior to deployment of the Licensed Software in a production environment, Channel Partner agrees to implement processes that will allow the Licensed Software to successfully report as specified in the Documentation. If Channel Partner fails to implement such processes within a reasonable time following iPass' request, iPass may elect to terminate the Agreement or Channel Partner's use of the Licensed Software upon written notice to Channel Partner. In such an event, Channel Partner shall be responsible for payment of all undisputed amounts due and owing to iPass under the Agreement. In addition, Channel Partner agrees to ensure that prior to deployment of the Licensed Software in a Business Entity production environment, each Business Entity implements processes that will allow the Licensed Software to successfully report as specified in the Documentation. If Channel Partner's Business Entity fails to implement such processes within a reasonable time following iPass' or Channel Partner's request, iPass may elect to terminate the order for such Business Entity upon written notice to Channel Partner. In such an event, Channel Partner shall be responsible for payment of all undisputed amounts due and owing to iPass under the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Services,' while the changed text replaces it with 'Deliverables.' The rest of the section (2. CHANNEL PARTNER RESPONSIBILITIES 2.1 General.) refers to Service, thus creating a contradiction as the replaced word Deliverables implies different obligations. The original 'Services' are specifically detailed elsewhere in the contract, and replacing it with the ambiguous term 'Deliverables' now introduces an inconsistency that would cause uncertainty about the channel partner's responsibilities.

perturbation.original_text: 3. SERVICE RATE PLAN 3.1 Fees. Channel Partner is solely responsible for billing End Users, and paying iPass for all usage of the Services. Channel Partner agrees to pay for usage of the Service on a monthly basis in accordance with the pricing set forth in Exhibit C. iPass may change the pricing in Exhibit C upon notice to Channel Partner, such changes to be effective within thirty (30) days after the date of such notice, such changes may include, by way of example only, the application of end of life fees.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. SERVICE RATE PLAN 3.1 Fees. Channel Partner is solely responsible for billing End Users, and paying iPass for all usage of the Products. Channel Partner agrees to pay for usage of the Product on a monthly basis in accordance with the pricing set forth in Exhibit C. iPass may change the pricing in Exhibit C upon notice to Channel Partner, such changes to be effective within thirty (30) days after the date of such notice, such changes may include, by way of example only, the application of end of life fees.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Services' with 'Products' introduces confusion regarding what the Channel Partner is actually paying for. 'Services' is defined, but 'Products' is not, so it becomes ambiguous whether the pricing covers software, support, or something else entirely. The term 'service' appears in SERVICE RATE PLAN, so it creats an in-text contradiction when changing 'Services' with 'Products' as well. This creates an in-text contradiction in section 3.1 as the term 'Service' is reffered to, in the rest of the legal text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. SERVICE RATE PLAN 3.1 Fees. Channel Partner is solely responsible for billing End Users, and paying iPass for all usage of the Products. Channel Partner agrees to pay for usage of the Product on a monthly basis in accordance with the pricing set forth in Exhibit C. iPass may change the pricing in Exhibit C upon notice to Channel Partner, such changes to be effective within thirty (30) days after the date of such notice, such changes may include, by way of example only, the application of end of life fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Services,' which is defined within the contract, establishing a clear subject for the agreement. The changed text replaces 'Services' with 'Products,' which is not defined, creating ambiguity and a direct conflict with the intent and understanding of what the Channel Partner is paying for, as the contract refers to 'Service Rate Plan' in the name, thus creating a conflict with the naming of the sections. This ambiguity directly contradicts the expectation of clarity and precision, causing potential disputes over contractual obligations and leading to Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, according to the Perturbation Definition.
